context.description: The frontend studio flow was hardened in Task 27 for server-side protection, but the role checks remained AUTHOR-only while Cluster 2 policy allows AUTHOR and EDITOR to access edit flows. Backend article and version management also still enforced AUTHOR-only logic.

problem.summary: EDITOR users were blocked from /studio and related article editing flows, even though the expected authorization policy allows AUTHOR or EDITOR access.

details.issue: Frontend server guard used AUTHOR-only logic for /studio.
Client draft studio guard duplicated the AUTHOR-only restriction.
Backend article management and version management services still enforced AUTHOR-only access.
Proxy initially blocked only unauthenticated users, so VIEWER users could still hit the streamed redirect path.

problem.expected_behavior: AUTHOR and EDITOR users should be able to access /studio and perform article edit operations. Unauthenticated users should be redirected to login before render. Authenticated but unauthorized users should still be blocked appropriately.
problem.actual_behavior: EDITOR users were rejected by frontend and backend AUTHOR-only checks. VIEWER users were not blocked early enough at the proxy layer for /studio.

possible_causes.cause: Role checks were copied into multiple frontend layers instead of using a shared AUTHOR or EDITOR helper.
Backend article and version services still reflected an older AUTHOR-only interpretation.
Proxy guard only checked cookie presence and not the role encoded in the access token payload.
Cluster 2 policy in prompt context was not fully propagated into implementation.

requirements.requirement: Add a shared helper for AUTHOR and EDITOR studio access
Use the shared helper in server and client studio guards
Allow AUTHOR and EDITOR roles in article management services
Allow EDITOR to manage all content while AUTHOR remains limited to own content
Apply the same rule to version history endpoints used by studio
Continue redirecting unauthenticated users to login
Decode JWT payload role in proxy for /studio only
Redirect unauthorized authenticated users away from /studio before render
Unauthenticated user is redirected to login
EDITOR can update an author-owned draft and fetch its versions

acceptance_criteria.criteria: AUTHOR can access /studio and perform edit operations
EDITOR can access /studio and perform edit operations
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login before render
Frontend and backend studio access rules are aligned
